title: Post-GWAS colocalization — the two-pillar flagship (walking skeleton)
description: The DATA + COMPUTE pillars composed on a real research question (post-GWAS colocalization, the shape of ~/PostGWAS + ~/coloclize), as DATA. The DAG: a GWAS locus and an eQTL-tissue locus are read into tables (file_scan; in production these are tabix region-extracts via duckhts), HARMONIZED in SQL (join on variant, align/flip alleles, drop mismatches, varbeta = se^2 — the DATA pillar), then COLOCALIZED out-of-process in R over Arrow IPC (coloc.abf — the COMPUTE pillar, a thing SQL is poor at) to yield the posteriors PP.H0..PP.H4. PP.H4 = probability the GWAS and eQTL share a causal variant. Every step leaves a receipt (resolver version, params digest, source snapshot / command digest). Walking skeleton: ONE locus + ONE tissue with coloc.abf (no LD needed); thickens to per-tissue fan-out + PLINK2 LD + SuSiE later.

resolvers.title: DuckDB file scan
resolvers.description: Read a delimited locus file into a table (stands in for a tabix region-extract).

resolvers.title: SQL materialize
resolvers.description: Materialize a table from a read-only SELECT (here, the allele harmonization join).

resolvers.title: Out-of-process compute
resolvers.description: Run an external process (R coloc.abf) over an Arrow-IPC input and read its Arrow-IPC output back as a table.

params.sql: SELECT e.tissue, g.snp, g.beta AS beta_gwas, g.se * g.se AS varbeta_gwas, CASE WHEN g.ea = e.ea AND g.oa = e.oa THEN e.beta WHEN g.ea = e.oa AND g.oa = e.ea THEN -e.beta END AS beta_eqtl, e.se * e.se AS varbeta_eqtl FROM gwas_locus g JOIN eqtl_locus e ON g.snp = e.snp WHERE (g.ea = e.ea AND g.oa = e.oa) OR (g.ea = e.oa AND g.oa = e.ea) ORDER BY e.tissue, g.pos

params.inputSql: SELECT tissue, snp, beta_gwas, varbeta_gwas, beta_eqtl, varbeta_eqtl FROM harmonized
